findings.evidence: Lines 74-79 add `variable [Fintype V]` immediately before `noncomputable def cubeVertices ... := S.powerset.image fun T => cubeVertex x T`; `Finset.image` needs decidable equality on the image type, while `Weight.lean:58-100` shows `[Fintype V]` is required later for `characteristicWeight`.
findings.fix: Move `cubeVertices`, `mem_cubeVertices`, `cubeVertices_nonempty`, `cubeVertices_subset`, etc. under `[DecidableEq (V → ℤ)]`, then reintroduce `[Fintype V]` only before the characteristic-weight declarations that use `P.characteristicWeight`.
findings.issue: `cubeVertices` and its elementary lemmas are placed under `[Fintype V]`, but the construction only images `S.powerset` into `V → ℤ`; finiteness of all of `V` is only being used to synthesize `DecidableEq (V → ℤ)`.

summary: The exposed cube-vertex API is mostly at the intended level, but the basic finite vertex-set layer assumes finite vertex types where Mathlib only needs decidable equality on the function target.
